channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: How the Ebola outbreak of 2014 was brought under control
item.description: Bringing the Ebola outbreak under control in 2014 was largely due to the extensive involvement from the U.S., through USAID, the White House, the CDC, and the military.
item.content:encoded: Bringing the Ebola outbreak under control in 2014 was largely due to the extensive involvement from the U.S., through USAID, the White House, the CDC, and the military.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Trump's endorsement in Republican Senate contest in Texas changes race in final days
item.description: A week out from the Texas Republican Senate runoff, President Trump endorsed Attorney General Ken Paxton over incumbent Sen. John Cornyn, as early voting is underway.
item.content:encoded: A week out from the Texas Republican Senate runoff, President Trump endorsed Attorney General Ken Paxton over incumbent Sen. John Cornyn, as early voting is underway.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Schneider

item.title: Voice actor Tom Kane, known for 'Powerpuff Girls' and 'Star Wars' universe, dies at 64
item.description: The work of voice actor Tom Kane attracted fans across generations and genres, from Cartoon Network's The Powerpuff Girls to the Star Wars universe. Kane died this week at age 64.
item.content:encoded: The work of voice actor Tom Kane attracted fans across generations and genres, from Cartoon Network's The Powerpuff Girls to the Star Wars universe. Kane died this week at age 64.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel J. Sánchez

item.title: Israel allows women to take rabbinic test for 1st time, but not become Orthodox rabbis
item.description: After years of court battles, Israel will allow women to take the exams men take to become rabbis. It is a step toward expanding women's leadership roles in Orthodox Judaism.
item.content:encoded: After years of court battles, Israel will allow women to take the exams men take to become rabbis. It is a step toward expanding women's leadership roles in Orthodox Judaism.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Visually impaired people get to enjoy rock climbing with help from a volunteer
item.description: A rock climber is using his skills to help those who are visually impaired to enjoy the sport as well.
item.content:encoded: A rock climber is using his skills to help those who are visually impaired to enjoy the sport as well.
item.dc:creator: Elvis Menayese

item.title: Kentucky Rep. Thomas Massie fights for his political career without Trump's backing
item.description: A Kentucky primary will test whether Rep. Thomas Massie, who has clashed with President Trump, has enough support among GOP voters in his district. Trump is backing GOP primary candidate Ed Gallrein.
item.content:encoded: A Kentucky primary will test whether Rep. Thomas Massie, who has clashed with President Trump, has enough support among GOP voters in his district. Trump is backing GOP primary candidate Ed Gallrein.
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Goodman

item.title: Democrats search for their policy agenda
item.description: A group of top Democrats from across the country met Tuesday to discuss the future of their agenda, beyond opposition to President Trump.
item.content:encoded: A group of top Democrats from across the country met Tuesday to discuss the future of their agenda, beyond opposition to President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Mara Liasson

item.title: World Cup bike lane sparks fury from Mexico City sex workers
item.description: A new World Cup bike lane in Mexico City is sparking outrage among sex workers, who say it's pushing them off the streets and threatening their livelihoods.
item.content:encoded: A new World Cup bike lane in Mexico City is sparking outrage among sex workers, who say it's pushing them off the streets and threatening their livelihoods.
item.dc:creator: Eyder Peralta

item.title: Cuba is out of oil. What now?
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Jorge Piñon, an energy researcher at the University of Texas at Austin and a former oil executive, about the energy crisis taking place in Cuba.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Jorge Piñon, an energy researcher at the University of Texas at Austin and a former oil executive, about the energy crisis taking place in Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Fiona Geiran

item.title: President Trump promised to house 6,000 homeless veterans in LA. His budget funds zero
item.description: One year after President Trump's executive order to build housing for thousands of homeless veterans in Los Angeles, advocates say little has been done.
item.content:encoded: One year after President Trump's executive order to build housing for thousands of homeless veterans in Los Angeles, advocates say little has been done.
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence
